transcription:
Lecture #7 ← a finite execution can show violation
- Safety: A bad thing won't happen
Liveness: A good thing eventually happens
        ↑ a finite execution can't show violation
snapshots are correct when:
    - They take consistent snapshots (safety)
    - The snapshot algorithm terminates (liveness)

Does C-L terminate?
    - Assumptions
      - Fixed number of processes

If every process ① records its own process state
          ② reads the states of all incoming channels
  C-L terminates.    ↖ finished when every channel
                       recieves a marker message

In C-L algo, at least one process has to initiate.

Multiple process initiation is actually a huge deal, becuz
if this wasn't true, it would be bad. Processes would
have to agree to decide which process gets to initiate.
       ↓        ↓
    not good   complexity

Decentralized algorithm: Can be independently
initiated by different processes without them needing
to coordinate with each other

Centralized algorithm: Must exactly have one
initiator

blocks.text: In C-L algo, at least one process has to initiate.
Multiple process initiation is actually a huge deal, becuz if this wasn't true, it would be bad. Processes would have to agree to decide which process gets to initiate.
Decentralized algorithm: Can be independently initiated by different processes without them needing to coordinate with each other
Centralized algorithm: Must exactly have one initiator
